title: Specify decision record front-matter
description: Validates the YAML front-matter block of a Decision Record (RFC-36). One schema validates both the slice-authored form under `.specify/slices/<slice>/decisions/<slug>.md` and the promoted baseline form under `.specify/decisions/DEC-NNNN-<slug>.md`. The slice authors `slug` plus `status: accepted | rejected` and optional `supersedes[]` / `related[]`; the engine stamps `id` / `slice` / `date` (and, on a superseded target, `status: superseded` + `superseded-by`) at merge. The engine-stamped fields are therefore optional here so the one schema serves both shapes; the persisted baseline form always carries `id` / `slice` / `date`.

slug.description: Stable kebab-case slug authored in the slice — the only key the agent writes. The engine derives the baseline filename `DEC-NNNN-<slug>.md` from it.

status.description: Lifecycle of the decision. The slice authors `accepted` or `rejected`; `superseded` is engine-only, stamped when a newer record names this one under `supersedes:`.

supersedes.description: Optional list of records this decision supersedes. Each entry is a baseline `DEC-NNNN` id or the `slug` of a record merged earlier in the same slice. Every target must resolve at merge or the merge aborts with `decision-supersede-orphan`.

related.description: Optional traceability into this slice's requirements (`REQ-NNN`).

id.description: Engine-stamped durable project-global id (`DEC-NNNN`). Absent in the slice-authored form; required on the persisted baseline form. Assigned by `specify slice merge` as `max(existing) + 1`, never reused.

slice.description: Engine-stamped slug of the slice that introduced the record. Absent in the slice-authored form.

date.description: Engine-stamped merge date (injected clock, `YYYY-MM-DD`). Absent in the slice-authored form.

superseded-by.description: Engine-stamped on a record whose `status` was flipped to `superseded`: the `DEC-NNNN` of the record that replaced it.
